## eDNA is Already in OBIS — and Being Used

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}

**Real eDNA datasets already published:**

- eDNA surveys from Monterey Bay, California
- 16S rRNA metabarcoding of pico-to-mesoplankton
- Fish eDNA surveys across multiple ocean basins

---

**How to find eDNA data in OBIS:**

```r
library(robis)

# Filter by DNA extension
occurrence(
  hasextensions = "DNADerivedData",
  taxonid = 127160  # e.g. Actinopteri
)
```

:::

::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px; margin-bottom:8px;"}
Species Distribution Modeling
:::
::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px; margin-bottom:8px;"}
Biodiversity Assessments
:::
::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px; margin-bottom:8px;"}
MPA Baseline Studies
:::
::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px;"}
Global Meta-analyses
:::

:::

::::

::: notes
There are already thousands of eDNA-derived occurrence records in OBIS. You can explore them via the OBIS Mapper by filtering for the DNADerivedData extension, or via robis with hasextensions='DNADerivedData'. These datasets are being used for species distribution modeling, biodiversity assessments, and baseline studies for marine protected areas.
:::
